references.title: Using Clickstream Data Mining Techniques to Understand and Support First-Generation College Students in an Online Chemistry Course

extracted.Features: [First Generation, Proportion of Video Visits, Proportion Late of Video Visits, Gender, Low-income status, UTS, SAT scores]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Clickstream logs are converted into the 2 SRL indicators used in this study. These are searated by Modules.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Group 1: Early Planning, 

Group 2: Planning, 

Group 3: Procrastination

Group 4: Low Engagement
extracted.Implications: Students in early planning received the highest grades in general… followed by Palnning, Procrastination and Low Engagement
extracted.Comments:
Authors use ANOVAs to compare differences between the resulting clusters.

Authors find significant differences among all of them.
extracted.Limitations:
k-Means only

Limited Access to LMS data

extracted.Preprocessing Details: Clickstream logs are converted into the 2 SRL indicators used in this study. These are searated by Modules.
extracted.Cluster Description: First-generation status had a significant negative contribution to course outcomes all else equal…
extracted.Implications: Students in early planning received the highest grades in general… followed by Palnning, Procrastination and Low Engagement
extracted.Comments:
Authors use ANOVAs to compare differences between the resulting clusters.

Limited Access to LMS data

annotations.text: MOOC success requires great amounts of SR skills. Authors look to understand self-regulated learning behaviors in n online chemistry course and a high percentage of first-generation students.
Authors use video data to create 2 distinct indicators of SRL: video completion and time management and then perform k-means clustering.
Authors find 4 groups: Early planning, Planning, Procrastination and Low Engagement and see that planning behavior is essencial towards success in the course.

Mapping clustering to first generation status, the authors found that First-Gen students with planning behaviors perform just as well as non first-gen. However, Firt-generation low engagement students were the lowest performing students.
